what_it_does: Defines a static 1600x900 HTML/CSS design board for an app canvas screen, with a phone mockup on the left and explanatory product-ground content on the right.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts, a shared vars.css file, and /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Centers a fixed-size .view artboard on the page with a warm left zone and a content column.
The left zone contains a generated mesh, a label, and a phone mockup with a radial background, status bar, frosted card, hotspots, dark glass controls, and a hatch strip.
The right column contains a superhead, headline, four fragment statements, a generated consequences box, a circular V mark, and a goldwash footer.
Inline JavaScript populates the mesh, icon bar, and consequences box through the global DNA.org API.
standouts: Phone UI is treated as a product-world canvas with floating chrome overlays.
The page pairs a rendered screen specimen with explicit design-principle fragments.
The hatch footer appears both inside the phone and across the outer design board.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties from the shared variable surface for spacing, type, color, radius, and line treatment.
Keeps all file-specific styling inline in the HTML document.
Uses absolute positioning for specimen details and fixed dimensions for the artboard and phone.
Uses generated organisms for repeated visual systems instead of writing those shapes directly in this file.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600x900 and is not responsive within this file.
The file depends on a global DNA object supplied by /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
Several visual positions and content values are hardcoded inline.
Root-relative asset paths require a compatible server context.
notes: This file is a self-contained design specimen for an app canvas screen where imagery acts as the primary surface and UI chrome floats above it.
questions: What environment supplies the /surface/styles/vars.css and /surface/runtime/organisms.js files?
Is this intended as a production screen mockup, a design-system specimen, or both?
Should the fixed artboard dimensions be preserved for all viewports?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: the file presents an app canvas-screen design board with a phone mockup and explanatory design principles.
apparent_themes: frosted and dark glass UI layers
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to define how an app canvas should feel: the product world occupies the main screen, while navigation, labels, hotspots, and explanation sit as layered chrome rather than framed panels.
novelty: The file combines a phone mockup, design manifesto text, generated system ornaments, and consequence accounting in a single fixed artboard.
contribution: Contributes a canvas-screen substrate with warm full-bleed product territory, floating dark-glass controls, frosted copy treatment, hotspot markers, hatch closure, and a paired explanatory ledger panel for use in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed HTML design board showing an app canvas screen with a warm phone-world mockup, floating UI chrome, hotspot markers, explanatory text fragments, and generated DNA organism graphics.
intention_for_embedding: This was for expressing a product-screen design principle where the world itself is the interface surface and supporting chrome floats above it.
